title: [wontfix] artifact: handle unnamed artifacts in list
body:
#### Checklist

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all commits.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #28033` in commit message
- [x] Tests have been added/updated (Verified with unit test in cmd/podman/artifact)
- [x] Documentation has been updated (No documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (Manual verification performed)
- [x] Release note entered in the section below

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Fix podman artifact ls and rm failing when artifacts lack the art-name annotation.
```

Fixes: #28033

While working on this PR, I realized that my initial approach (handling unnamed artifacts in ls and rm) was really just a workaround for the underlying issue you pointed out.

Since pull should be in charge of naming, I decided to rewrite the implementation completely. Instead of adding defensive code to tolerate unnamed artifacts, I've updated artifact pull to validate that the pulled object is actually a valid OCI artifact (checking for required annotations).

Now, if you try to pull a non-artifact image like Alpine, it fails immediately with a clear error instead of creating a broken unnamed artifact.

I've force-pushed this new approach which I believe is the correct fix.
is the desired behavior that one can pull something one cannot work with?  also, if there is broad agreement on this, should it go into `container-libs` ?

> is the desired behavior that one can pull something one cannot work with? also, if there is broad agreement on this, should it go into `container-libs` ?

I don’t see a _strong_ reason why `podman artifact` should entirely reject working with images. (OTOH 2 weak reasons:
- It’s fairly likely the user made a mistake
- Images don’t have the `…unknown` “file name” annotations on each layer … but I think `podman artifact` should be useful for other kinds of artifacts as well, to a fair extent

)

---

I still haven’t see any explanation for how it happens that a pulled object can influence the name in the local store (or at least to cause it to be unnamed). I think this is critical to be understood prior to discussing any implementation proposals. At some point I’m going to stop asking and just close this.

@baude Do you see any logic in this?

I’m out of patience with the non-diagnoses and planning to just close — do you happen to see where I’m missing something?

The diagnosis is a mismatch between the input reference and OCI Layout constraints. When podman artifact pull is given a full URI (e.g., quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest), the underlying c/image library attempts to store this as the org.opencontainers.image.ref.name annotation. However, per OCI specs, this annotation should only be a tag, not a full URI.

Consequently, the backend silently omits the invalid name to maintain OCI compliance, leading to an 'unnamed' artifact in the local store. This is why handling unnamed objects in list and rm is necessary: it's a structural side-effect of using OCI Layouts with full registry references

@kairosci Did actually run these commands?

```
podman artifact pull quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest
podman artifact ls
```
works just fine for me?!
Neither the PR nor the issue is actually proving a reproducer for maintainers to understand what problem here should be. Without this it does seems pointless for maintainers to further engage on this.

```
podman artifact rm quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest
```

works just fine as well, and besides that would move the goal post of the issue which talks about list, the code changes pull, and you talk about rm failing?

Like at this point I see no reason to continue looking at this as you still have not addressed the critical question how is the `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` annotation missing as pull always sets that.

And regardless fixing pull here here on the podman side is wrong, if there must be a behavior change how pull stores names then it would have to happen on the in the libartifact code not here as that code has several consumers not just podman.
But again please do not open a PR there unless you can demonstrate the actual bug problem here
